seoTitle: How to Change Language on Coursera: Settings Guide
metaDescription: Want to use Coursera in a different language? Here's how to find and change your language settings, plus tips for checking course availability.
intro: Switching Coursera's interface to your preferred language can make a real difference in how well you follow along with course content, especially for complex subjects. This guide covers locating the language settings and walking through the change step by step. It's aimed at students and professionals who want a more comfortable, accessible learning experience.

steps.heading: Access Coursera's account settings
steps.body: Language preferences live within your account settings, so start there to make any changes.

steps.heading: Change your language preference
steps.body: Select your preferred language from the available options and apply the change to your account.

steps.heading: Understand the benefits of language customization
steps.body: Using the platform in your preferred language can improve comprehension and make learning feel more accessible.

steps.heading: Check course availability in your selected language
steps.body: Not all courses may be available in every language, so verify before enrolling that your chosen course supports it.

faq.question: Can I change Coursera's interface language after signing up?
faq.answer: Yes, language settings can be changed at any time from within your account settings.

faq.question: Does changing my language setting affect which courses I can take?
faq.answer: It's worth checking course availability in your selected language, since not every course may be offered in every language Coursera supports.

faq.question: Is Coursera available in languages other than English?
faq.answer: Yes, Coursera supports multiple languages to make learning more accessible to a global audience of students and professionals.

faq.question: Will switching languages log me out of my account?
faq.answer: No, changing your language preference is a settings adjustment and shouldn't affect your account access or progress.
